course.title: HTML & CSS Fundamentals
course.description: Learn the building blocks of the web: HTML for structure and CSS for styling.

chapter.title: Intermediate CSS: Classes and IDs
chapter.description: Learn how to target specific elements with CSS classes and IDs for advanced styling.

lessons.title: Using CSS Classes
objectives: Apply styles to multiple elements using classes
Understand the difference between classes and inline styles
lessons.explanation: A CSS class allows you to define a style once and apply it to multiple elements. In HTML, you use the `class` attribute. In your `<style>` block, you target a class using a period (`.`) followed by the class name.
lessons.syntax:
This text is highlighted.

This one is too!

exercises.statement:
Create a CSS class called `.alert` that sets the text color to red. Apply this class to a paragraph `

` that says 'Danger!'.
exercises.starter_code: Danger!
exercises.hint:
In the style block, write .alert { color: red; }. Then add class="alert" to the

tag.

lessons.title: Using CSS IDs
objectives: Apply styles to a unique element using IDs
Understand when to use IDs vs Classes
lessons.explanation: A CSS ID is used to style a single, unique element on a page (an ID should only be used once per page). In HTML, you use the `id` attribute. In CSS, you target an ID using a hash (`#`) followed by the ID name.
lessons.syntax: My Website Header

exercises.statement: Create a CSS ID called `#main-title` that sets the font-size to 40px. Apply this ID to an `<h1>` tag that says 'Welcome'.
exercises.starter_code: Welcome
exercises.hint: In the style block, write #main-title { font-size: 40px; }. Then add id="main-title" to the <h1> tag.
